manuscript-body.purpose: Long-form scholarly prose in LaTeX and Word ports.

figure-title.purpose: One concise claim-framing sentence inside generated figures.

code-label.purpose: Metric names, action contracts, seeds, and artifact paths.

components.name: Manuscript Figure Node
components.description: A labeled thesis/DES operation or metric node in generated manuscript figures.
components.css: .ds-figure-node { display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 120px; min-height: 54px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #0072B2; border-radius: 6px; background: #FFFFFF; color: #1F2933; font-family: STIXGeneral, DejaVu Serif, serif; text-align: center; } .ds-figure-node strong { font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.05; } .ds-figure-node span { font-size: 10px; line-height: 1.15; }

components.name: Boundary Callout
components.description: A compact warning label for severe-regime or claim-boundary evidence.
components.css: .ds-boundary-callout { display: inline-block; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #D55E00; border-radius: 8px; background: #FFFFFF; color: #D55E00; font-family: STIXGeneral, DejaVu Serif, serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Evidence Table
components.description: A booktabs-style compact evidence table for metric/comparator panels.
components.css: .ds-evidence-table { border-collapse: collapse; color: #1F2933; font-family: Latin Modern Roman, Computer Modern, Times New Roman, serif; font-size: 12px; } .ds-evidence-table th, .ds-evidence-table td { padding: 6px 8px; text-align: left; } .ds-evidence-table thead { border-top: 1.2px solid #1F2933; border-bottom: 0.8px solid #1F2933; } .ds-evidence-table tbody tr:last-child { border-bottom: 1.2px solid #1F2933; }

narrative.overview: The manuscript should read like a precise engineering brief, not a promotional AI paper. Its visual system exists to make the evidence chain hard to miss: Garrido-Rios thesis lineage, final Q1 evidence bundle, comparator hierarchy, uncertainty, and claim boundary.
keyCharacteristics: No motion or decorative AI/SaaS tropes

rules.name: The Semantic Color Rule
rules.body: Colors must encode meaning, not decoration.

rules.name: The Boundary Is Visible Rule
rules.body: Severe/h52 and other claim boundaries must be marked with redundant labels.

rules.name: The No Hero Type Rule
rules.body: This is a journal article, not a landing page.

rules.name: The Print-First Rule
rules.body: If a figure depends on shadow, blur, or transparency to communicate structure, redesign it.

dos: Do make every visual claim trace to the final Q1 stats bundle or named E1-E6 artifact.
Do keep Track A boundary evidence and Track B positive evidence visually distinct.
donts: Don't use perfect-fill, zero-backorder, 57% cost-win, universal RL-law, anticipation, retained-learning, or closed-generalization wording.
Don't encode findings by color alone.
